questions.q: Who directed the 1964 Bengali film Charulata?
questions.explanation: Charulata (1964) was written and directed by Satyajit Ray.

questions.q: In which year was Satyajit Ray's film Charulata released?
questions.explanation: Charulata was released in 1964.

questions.q: The film Charulata is based on a novella by which Bengali literary figure?
questions.explanation: Charulata is adapted from Rabindranath Tagore's novella 'Nastanirh' (The Broken Nest).

questions.q: What is the title of the Rabindranath Tagore novella on which Charulata is based?
questions.explanation: Charulata is based on Tagore's novella 'Nastanirh', meaning 'The Broken Nest'.

questions.q: Which actress plays the title role of Charulata in Satyajit Ray's 1964 film?
questions.explanation: Madhabi Mukherjee plays Charulata, the lonely wife at the centre of the film.

questions.q: The subtitle by which Charulata is often known in English is which of the following?
questions.explanation: Charulata is widely known in English as 'The Lonely Wife'.

questions.q: In which language was Satyajit Ray's film Charulata made?
questions.explanation: Charulata was made in Bengali.

questions.q: In roughly which historical period is the story of Charulata set?
questions.explanation: Charulata is set in an upper-class Bengali household in the late 19th century, around the 1870s-1880s.

questions.q: Who composed the music for Satyajit Ray's film Charulata?
questions.explanation: Satyajit Ray himself composed the music for Charulata, as he did for most of his films from the early 1960s onward.

questions.q: Charulata, the central character in Ray's 1964 film, is portrayed as a woman who is which of the following?
questions.explanation: Charulata is a cultured but neglected wife who fills her lonely hours with reading and writing.

questions.q: Which actor plays Bhupati, Charulata's busy newspaper-editor husband, in the 1964 film?
questions.explanation: Sailen Mukherjee plays Bhupati, the politically engaged newspaper editor and Charulata's husband.

questions.q: Which actor plays Amal, the young cousin who awakens Charulata's affections, in Ray's Charulata?
questions.explanation: Soumitra Chatterjee plays Amal, Bhupati's literary-minded young cousin.

questions.q: What profession occupies Bhupati, Charulata's husband, keeping him absorbed away from his wife?
questions.explanation: Bhupati runs and edits an English-language political newspaper called 'The Sentinel', which consumes his attention.

questions.q: At the 1965 Berlin International Film Festival, what award did Satyajit Ray win for Charulata?
questions.explanation: Ray won the Silver Bear for Best Director for Charulata at the 1965 Berlin Film Festival.

questions.q: An iconic early sequence in Charulata shows the heroine observing the outside world through what object?
questions.explanation: In the celebrated opening, a bored Charulata watches passers-by through a pair of opera glasses from behind window shutters.

questions.q: Charulata is frequently described by Satyajit Ray himself as which of the following regarding his own filmography?
options: A film he wished he had never made
The film closest to flawless among his works
questions.explanation: Ray often remarked that Charulata was the one film of his that came closest to being flawless.

questions.q: Which famous garden sequence is a celebrated set-piece in Charulata, showing the heroine's emotions through movement?
options: A boat ride down the Ganges
A folk dance in the courtyard
A horse ride through the city
The swing (jhula) scene in the garden
questions.explanation: The swing sequence, in which Charulata sways on a garden swing while observing Amal, is among the film's most renowned moments.

questions.q: Who was the cinematographer for Satyajit Ray's Charulata?
questions.explanation: Subrata Mitra, Ray's long-time cinematographer, shot Charulata in black and white.

questions.q: Both Charulata and Amal share a passion that draws them together in the film. What is it?
questions.explanation: Charulata and Amal bond over a shared love of Bengali literature and creative writing.

questions.q: In which format was Satyajit Ray's Charulata shot?
questions.explanation: Charulata was shot in black and white, as were most of Ray's films of that era.

questions.q: Madhabi Mukherjee, who plays Charulata, had earlier worked with Satyajit Ray on which 1963 film?
questions.explanation: Madhabi Mukherjee starred in Ray's Mahanagar (1963) before playing Charulata the following year.

questions.q: The famous freeze-frame ending of Charulata shows the hands of Charulata and Bhupati doing what?
options: Reaching toward but not quite clasping each other
questions.explanation: The film ends on a freeze-frame of the couple's hands reaching toward one another but stopping short, leaving their reconciliation unresolved.

questions.q: Amal sings a Tagore song in Charulata that becomes a memorable moment. Which song is most associated with that scene?
questions.explanation: Amal sings the Rabindra Sangeet 'Phule Phule Dhole Dhole' in the celebrated garden swing sequence.

questions.q: Bhupati's newspaper in Charulata, which absorbs his political idealism, is named what?
questions.explanation: Bhupati edits a pro-liberal English newspaper called 'The Sentinel' in the film.

questions.q: Bhupati is financially betrayed in Charulata by a close relative who manages his finances. Who is that character?
questions.explanation: Umapada, Charulata's brother who helped manage the newspaper's accounts, embezzles funds and flees, devastating Bhupati.

questions.q: In Charulata, which strand of European politics does the newspaper editor Bhupati follow with great enthusiasm?
options: British Liberal Party and Gladstone-era politics
questions.explanation: Bhupati is an admirer of British liberal politics, following Gladstone and the Liberal Party in his newspaper's editorial stance.

questions.q: Charulata's own literary talent is revealed in the film when she writes and publishes a piece in a Bengali magazine. What subject does her writing draw upon?
questions.explanation: Charulata secretly writes a nostalgic prose piece about her rural childhood and gets it published, surprising Amal.

questions.q: Which of these statements about the relationship between Amal and Charulata in the film is accurate?
options: Amal is Bhupati's younger cousin and a brother-in-law figure to Charulata
questions.explanation: Amal is Bhupati's younger cousin who comes to stay with the family, making him a brother-in-law figure to Charulata.

questions.q: The English title 'The Broken Nest' refers to the meaning of which word connected to Charulata's source material?
questions.explanation: Tagore's novella 'Nastanirh', the film's source, literally translates as 'The Broken Nest'.

questions.q: Charulata is widely regarded by critics as belonging to which strand of Satyajit Ray's work?
options: His refined adaptations of Tagore set in 19th-century Bengal
questions.explanation: Charulata stands as one of Ray's finest period adaptations of Rabindranath Tagore, set in late 19th-century Bengal.
